findings.evidence: Existing API: `code/TauCeti/Analysis/PositiveDefinite/Kernel.lean:74` defines `IsPositiveDefiniteKernel`; `:211` gives the arbitrary finite quadratic-form characterization; `:85`, `:90`, `:107`, `:117`, and `code/TauCeti/Analysis/PositiveDefinite/KernelClosure.lean:53` provide diagonal nonnegativity, conjugate symmetry, addition, nonnegative scalar multiplication, and finite sums. New duplicate finite form is at `SemigroupGroup.lean:58-60`.
findings.fix: Define this as an abbreviation/definition in terms of `IsPositiveDefiniteKernel (fun p q : ℝ≥0 × V => F (p.1 + q.1, p.2 - q.2))`, add only a finite-form iff/restatement if needed, and derive `map_zero_nonneg`, `conj_symm`, `add`, `const_mul`, and `sum` from the existing kernel API.
findings.issue: `IsSemigroupGroupPD` is the existing kernel predicate applied to `K p q = F (p.1 + q.1, p.2 - q.2)`, but the PR redefines the finite quadratic-form predicate and then reproves kernel facts under a parallel namespace.

summary: The PR duplicates TauCeti's existing positive-definite kernel predicate and its API instead of specializing it to the BCR kernel.
